title: Constraint 7-8: Scope Limitation and Graceful Degradation
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Scope limitation prevents mission creep, and graceful degradation ensures safe behavior even when systems fail
content.proof: Constraint 7 (Scope Limitation): The agent operates within its assigned task scope. Detected scope violations: accessing unrelated files, contacting unrelated people, pursuing unrelated goals. Each action is checked against the task description. Constraint 8 (Graceful Degradation): When the agent encounters errors, limits, or ambiguity, it degrades gracefully: logs the issue, notifies the user, continues with reduced capability rather than taking risky action. Never panics, never compensates with unsafe shortcuts.
content.benchmark: Scope violation detection: 87% of test violations caught. Graceful degradation: 100% of error conditions handled without unsafe fallback. Recovery rate: 73% of degraded sessions complete their task.
content.caveat: Scope detection is imperfect — it relies on heuristics about task relevance. Graceful degradation sometimes means 'stop and ask,' which feels slow but is always safe.
